title: hieracium subcaesium subsp. crispans
intro: Hieracium subcaesium subsp. crispans, also known as the Meadow Hawkweed, is a plant species in the dandelion family (Asteraceae) characterized by its yellow flowers and narrow leaves. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects. The health benefits of Hieracium subcaesium subsp. crispans include treating wounds, reducing fever, and alleviating digestive issues. It is often used in herbal preparations such as infusions, tinctures, and salves, although its use should be approached with caution due to potential toxicity and lack of scientific evidence.
